about.title: An investment advisor with a high-performance mindset.
about.body1: Mathieu Poissonnet is a Dubai-based real estate advisor focused on off-plan investment opportunities across the UAE. He advises private investors, high-net-worth families, and developers — combining deep market research, direct developer access, and structured financing solutions to make each transaction seamless and each investment thesis defensible.
about.body2: Before real estate, Mathieu spent seven years in elite gymnastics coaching in Wales, where results are measurable and preparation is everything. That same discipline now shapes every mandate: research first, structure second, close cleanly — no hype, no shortcuts, no wasted meetings.
about.credential3: Previously at Huspy, BWC Real Estate, Harvey Smith
about.placeholderBadge: Illustrative figures — final numbers on request

page.title: A quiet advisor for people who don't want to shop in public.
page.intro1: Mathieu Poissonnet advises private investors, families and developers on Dubai real estate — with a focus on off-plan opportunities and structured deals that don't turn up on portal listings.
page.intro2: Every mandate starts with a written brief, a research memo and a modelled exit before a single unit is discussed. No hype, no shortcuts, no fifty-slide developer decks.
page.philosophyBody:
Before real estate, I spent seven years in elite gymnastics coaching in Wales. Preparation was everything; results were measurable to the tenth of a point.

That's how I approach a mandate now. Research first, structure second, close cleanly. If the numbers don't work, I say so — and then I find something that does.

gymnastics.company: Welsh Gymnastics · Cardiff Central YC · Capital Gymnastics

page.languagesBody: French (native) · English (fluent). Everything from first message to signing translates seamlessly across both.

listings.title: A shortlist worth a conversation.
listings.subtitle: A few of the mandates on the desk this quarter. Full portfolio — off-plan launches, resales, and off-market villas — available on request.

palm.name: Palm Jumeirah — Signature Villa
palm.blurb: Six-bedroom beachfront villa with private pool, cinema and 30m of Arabian Gulf frontage. Ready secondary-market opportunity, immediate handover.
palm.description:
This is one of a handful of villas built to the original 2007 palm-frond specification with three-storey volume — the ceilings are what everyone notices first. The layout gives you a formal entertaining floor at ground level, all bedrooms and family living on the first, and a rooftop deck on the second.

Thirty metres of private beach, a linear infinity pool along the beach line, and staff quarters accessed through a separate service entrance. The current owner completed a full renovation in 2023 — smart-home, all bathrooms, kitchen — and the villa comes fully furnished.

Representing the seller directly, off-market. Financials on request under NDA.

downtown.name: Emaar Beachfront — Off-Plan Penthouse
downtown.blurb: Four-bedroom off-plan penthouse in Emaar's flagship waterfront community. Priority allocation, staged payment plan, handover Q4 2028.
downtown.description:
Emaar Beachfront is Emaar's masterplanned peninsula between JBR and the Palm — the last piece of waterfront land inside central Dubai. This penthouse sits on the top floor of a west-facing tower, with an uninterrupted Palm Jumeirah view and a wraparound terrace.

Off-plan release with priority allocation for a limited handful of buyers. Payment plan is 60% during construction, 40% on handover Q4 2028 — the tightest schedule Emaar has committed to on the peninsula.

Unit is unfurnished, delivered white-box with premium finishes. Ideal for buyers who want to time construction to a specific relocation window.

hills.name: Emirates Hills — Custom Mansion
hills.blurb: Seven-bedroom custom-built mansion on the 9th fairway, staff wing, indoor pool and full smart-home. Ready keys, off-market.
hills.description:
Custom-built mansion on one of the last available plots in Sector W of Emirates Hills — the golf-facing sector considered the community's most prestigious address. Seven bedrooms across the main house, plus a separate staff wing with three additional rooms.

Direct frontage onto the 9th fairway of the Montgomerie. Indoor pool, private cinema, wine cellar for 900 bottles, and a fully-integrated smart-home spec that manages climate, security, and lighting from a single interface.

Representing the owner in a discreet off-market process. Introductions to qualified buyers only.

index.title: Current selection.

detail.askAbout: I'd like to know more about

index.title: Where I work.
index.subtitle: Short guides to the Dubai neighbourhoods I know inside out — the market fundamentals, the lifestyle, and what's actually worth buying.

palm-jumeirah.name: Palm Jumeirah
palm-jumeirah.summary: Dubai's iconic seven-mile palm-shaped island. Family villas on the fronds, apartments and hotels along the trunk.
palm-jumeirah.lifestyle:
Palm Jumeirah is where Dubai's oceanfront villa market begins. Owners tend to be end-users or long-term holders — the frond villas are the most protected asset in the city thanks to permanent supply constraints.

Expect quiet family streets on the fronds, a bustling trunk with retail and F&B, and a growing luxury hotel presence at the crescent tip. Access is via the Palm Monorail, the trunk road, or private boat.

nakheel-mall.name: Nakheel Mall
nakheel-mall.description: The Palm's central mall with a top-floor viewing deck across the entire crescent.

atlantis.name: Atlantis The Palm & The Royal
atlantis.description: Twin luxury resort anchors at the crescent tip — Nobu, Ossiano, Aquaventure.

private-beaches.name: Private beaches
private-beaches.description: Every frond villa has its own private beach frontage on the Arabian Gulf.

the-view.name: The View at The Palm
the-view.description: 240-metre observation deck at the top of the Palm Tower with 360° views of the island.

supply.title: Fixed supply
supply.description: No new frond villa land will be created — supply is fixed at the original 2007 masterplan.

yield.title: Yield & capital growth
yield.description: Prime frond villas have compounded at 12–18% per annum over the last five years with rental yields around 4–5%.

downtown-dubai.name: Downtown Dubai
downtown-dubai.summary: Home of the Burj Khalifa and Dubai Fountain — the city's most recognisable skyline and its most walkable luxury district.
downtown-dubai.lifestyle:
Downtown is where central Dubai happens. Boulevard-facing towers by Emaar are the reference product — Armani, Address, Burj Vista, Il Primo — and the entire district was designed as a walkable retail-hotel-residential grid.

Best suited to investors and end-users who want restaurants, art, and business districts all inside a fifteen-minute walk. Off-plan supply from Emaar continues to lead pricing.

burj-khalifa.name: Burj Khalifa
burj-khalifa.description: The world's tallest building anchors the district — visible from every north-facing residence.

dubai-mall.name: The Dubai Mall
dubai-mall.description: The world's largest mall with a direct residents' walkway from most Downtown towers.

fountain.name: Dubai Fountain
fountain.description: The 275-metre choreographed fountain — nightly shows draw crowds to the boulevard.

opera.name: Dubai Opera
opera.description: The 2,000-seat multi-format concert and opera venue on the boulevard.

walkability.title: Rare Dubai walkability
walkability.description: One of only two Dubai districts where daily life works entirely on foot — Downtown and DIFC.

brand.title: Brand asset
brand.description: Address, Armani and Il Primo units carry a permanent brand premium that resists softening cycles.

emirates-hills.name: Emirates Hills
emirates-hills.summary: Dubai's answer to Beverly Hills — a fully gated community of custom mansions built around the Montgomerie golf course.
emirates-hills.lifestyle:
Emirates Hills is the top of Dubai's villa market. Everything is custom-built on plots between 12,000 and 40,000 sq ft, gated at three checkpoints, with a private international school and country club inside the perimeter.

Ownership skews toward end-users with generational holding horizons. Deals are almost entirely off-market — the Sector W plots on the 9th fairway are among the most tightly-held addresses in the UAE.

montgomerie-golf.name: The Montgomerie Golf Club
montgomerie-golf.description: 18-hole Colin Montgomerie–designed championship course wrapping the community.

lake-views.name: Central lake
lake-views.description: The community-wide lake system with prime W-Sector plots facing directly onto it.

gated-community.name: Three-gate security
gated-community.description: Three separate manned checkpoints with 24/7 patrols — one of Dubai's most secure addresses.

international-schools.name: Dubai International Academy
international-schools.description: IB-curriculum school directly inside the Emirates Hills perimeter.

supply.title: Ultra-limited supply
supply.description: Only 620 plots exist inside the perimeter — most trade off-market and rarely.

prestige.title: Prestige premium
prestige.description: The Sector W address commands a 30–50% premium over comparable custom villa communities.

index.title: Live projects worth watching.
index.subtitle: A short list of active developments across the UAE — briefed with the same discipline as a private mandate: developer, location, product, price signal, timeline.

hq-by-rove.name: HQ by Rove — Marasi Bay
hq-by-rove.location: Marasi Bay Marina, Business Bay · Dubai
hq-by-rove.summary: The region's first hospitality-branded office building — 23 office floors on Marasi Bay Marina, 120,000 sq ft of amenities, and an HQ Desk concierge in partnership with Cushman & Wakefield Core.
hq-by-rove.overview:
HQ by Rove is IRTH Urban Development's answer to the tired glass-tower office — a next-generation, hospitality-led concept that integrates work, life, and play. Set on a 76,000 sq ft plot at Marasi Bay Marina in Business Bay, the 23-office-floor building carries the Rove Hotels brand — Rove being the Emaar Properties × Dubai Holding joint venture — and treats the workplace the way Rove treats a hotel: 24/7 service, concierge, and a curated amenity core baked into the lease. Fully-furnished and fully-serviced modular offices for every team size sit alongside Grade-A commercial floor plates.

That amenity core runs to 120,000 sq ft — 28% of the entire project — stretching across a dedicated podium ("HQ Land"), a mid-tower amenity floor ("Reset 16"), a rooftop "OOO" observation deck, an arcade level with private road access, and a lobby-level "Hub" and food hall ("The Block"). Padel and basketball courts, a fully-fitted gym, a yoga studio, a podcast cabin, floating treehouse pods, a semi-Olympic pool with spa/steam/sauna, event spaces, and a beauty lounge sit alongside 14 destination-controlled high-speed passenger lifts and 988 parking bays. HQ owners also plug into Rove Connect — preferential rates across Rove Hotels and cafés, priority access to future Rove-branded launches, and enterprise-class concierge services delivered through the HQ Desk in partnership with Cushman & Wakefield Core. Marasi Bay itself is set to host the first beach club in the Burj Khalifa District.
hq-by-rove.paymentPlan: Mortgage-friendly plan direct from IRTH: 15% down payment, then 5% on 1 November 2025, 5% on 1 April 2026, 5% on 1 September 2026, 5% on 1 February 2027, 5% at 40% construction (on or before 1 December 2027), 5% at 60% construction (on or before 1 May 2028), 5% at 80% construction (on or before 1 November 2028), and the final 50% on completion. Anticipated handover Q1 2029.

hospitality-brand.title: Hospitality-led workplace
hospitality-brand.description: Operated the way Rove Hotels are operated — 24/7 service, concierge, F&B and events layered under the office lease. Rove is Emaar × Dubai Holding's joint venture brand, extended here into workplace for the first time.

amenities-120k.title: 120,000 sq ft of amenities — 28% of the project
amenities-120k.description: One of the largest single-building amenity programmes in Dubai — fully-fitted gym, semi-Olympic pool, spa/steam/sauna pods, food hall ("The Block"), padel and basketball courts, podcast cabin, yoga space, event spaces, floating treehouse pods, and a rooftop observation deck. Sized so members almost never need to leave the building.

lifestyle-mix.title: 23 office floors, up to 22 modular units per floor
lifestyle-mix.description: Product ranges from studio-sized modular offices to Simplex full-floor plates and Penthouse Lofts. 14 destination-controlled high-speed passenger lifts, 988 parking bays across 8 floors, dedicated EV charging and bicycle parking.

rooftop-deck.title: HQ Desk concierge with Cushman & Wakefield Core
rooftop-deck.description: IRTH's strategic partnership with Cushman & Wakefield Core delivers enterprise-class concierge inside the building — professional call handling, dedicated account manager, IT support, meeting-room bookings, and mail/courier management for tenants of every size.

palm-jebel-ali.name: Palm Jebel Ali
palm-jebel-ali.summary: Nakheel's revived crown jewel — a palm-shaped megaproject twice the scale of Palm Jumeirah, redesigned as an exclusive collection of 5-to-7-bedroom beachfront villas from 7,316 sq ft, freehold for all nationalities.
palm-jebel-ali.overview:
Nakheel Properties has taken Palm Jebel Ali off its long pause and re-cast it as the reference beachfront villa product for the next decade. The master plan runs to seven fronds and a trunk, each frond effectively a private beach community wrapped in Nakheel's own infrastructure, retail and hotel commitments. Villas are sold as an exclusive collection of 5, 6 and 7-bedroom homes, freehold for all nationalities, starting from 7,316 sq ft, with released colourways including Azure Blue, Indigo Ocean, Blue Horizon, Ruby Sunset and Red Aurora.

Execution is now visibly on the ground. In April 2026, Nakheel awarded AED 3.5 billion in construction contracts covering 544 villas across the earliest fronds, with roughly AED 750 million of separate infrastructure works — roads, utilities, power distribution, telecoms — targeted for completion by the end of 2026. Sales have proceeded in phased releases under a 20% on booking · 80% during construction payment structure, per the broker page.
palm-jebel-ali.handoverText: Initial residences anticipated end of 2027 · Q4 2028 for the 544-villa contract phase awarded in 2026
palm-jebel-ali.priceSourceNote: Indicative broker-listed information; subject to change.
palm-jebel-ali.paymentPlan: 20% on booking · 80% during construction, as listed on the All Properties broker page. Nakheel's own site does not currently publish a public schedule, and terms have varied per release; the applicable plan is confirmed with Nakheel at time of registration.

master-plan-scale.title: Twice the scale of Palm Jumeirah
master-plan-scale.description: Seven fronds and a trunk — the master plan is roughly double the footprint of the original Palm, giving Nakheel room to redesign around private beach frontage rather than density. Villas start at 7,316 sq ft.

beach-frontage.title: Private beach per frond villa
beach-frontage.description: Frond villas front their own private stretches of Gulf beach and interior lagoons, with dining pavilions and infinity pools engineered around the waterline — the same product feature that made Palm Jumeirah's frond villas the most protected asset in Dubai.

payment-plan-20-80.title: 20% on booking · 80% during construction
payment-plan-20-80.description: Broker-listed payment structure keeps the buyer's exposure loaded during the build window rather than at handover — improving deployment IRR on unfinished stock. Freehold for all nationalities.

nakheel-master-plan.title: Nakheel-controlled infrastructure
nakheel-master-plan.description: Roads, utilities and community infrastructure are Nakheel Properties' own delivery — not a third-party contractor's — which historically shortens the gap between infrastructure handover and the first residents moving in. Amenities across the master plan include tennis courts, community pools, jogging tracks and children's play areas.

bayn-by-ora.name: Bayn by ORA
bayn-by-ora.summary: ORA Developers' master-planned beachfront community on the Ghantoot coast — a full destination pairing 3–5 BR villas and mansions with a private beach, marina, lagoons, retail, hospitality and a published 60/40 payment plan.
bayn-by-ora.overview:
Bayn by ORA is a master-planned luxury coastal community on the Ghantoot coast — the strip that connects Abu Dhabi to Dubai and has historically been reserved for royal and government uses. ORA Developers has positioned it as a full destination rather than a residential enclave: beach and marina alongside lagoons, parks, retail, schools, healthcare and a resort-led hospitality component, designed to support consistent end-user demand rather than pure investor churn.

The launch release is an exclusive collection of 3-to-5-bedroom beachfront villas and mansions plus a limited number of plots, sized for buyers who want a primary or secondary residence with resort-grade amenity and single-hop access to both emirates. The site sits 7 minutes from Palm Jebel Ali, 15 minutes from Al Maktoum International Airport, and 30 minutes from both Downtown Dubai and Downtown Abu Dhabi. Starting price is on request; a 60/40 payment plan — 10% at booking, 50% during construction, 40% on handover — is published as the standard structure.
bayn-by-ora.paymentPlan: 60/40 published by ORA — 10% down payment on booking, 50% in flexible installments during construction, and the final 40% on handover.

private-beach.title: Private beach & waterfront promenade
private-beach.description: The community fronts its own stretch of Ghantoot coastline with a private beach, waterfront promenade, and walking, jogging and cycling tracks — a rare piece of buildable UAE coastline that isn't already an occupied resort.

marina.title: Marina & yacht berthing
marina.description: An on-community marina with yacht berthing facilities removes the drive to Dubai Marina, Mina Rashid or Yas — a hard-to-replicate amenity for boat-owning end-users on the Ghantoot corridor.

lagoon-living.title: Lagoon living & a full-destination masterplan
lagoon-living.description: Internal water features and lagoon-fronting villas extend the waterfront experience inland. Bayn is planned as a full destination — retail and dining, community essentials, parks, schools, healthcare and a resort-led hospitality component — rather than a residential enclave.

location-between-emirates.title: Between Dubai and Abu Dhabi
location-between-emirates.description: 7 min to Palm Jebel Ali · 15 min to Al Maktoum International Airport · 30 min to Downtown Dubai · 30 min to Downtown Abu Dhabi. Ghantoot puts residents inside the daily commute range of both emirates without committing to either — useful for buyers whose lives split between the two.

index.title: Market notes.
index.subtitle: Long-form thinking on Dubai off-plan launches, community fundamentals, and investment structuring — written between mandates, not before them.

detail.ctaHeading: Have a specific case in mind?
detail.ctaBody: Send a short brief and I'll come back with a modelled shortlist.

palm-jebel-ali.title: Why Palm Jebel Ali is the most watched off-plan launch of 2026
palm-jebel-ali.excerpt: Nakheel's revived mega-project is priced to move. What the payment plan actually says about who they want as buyers.
palm-jebel-ali.lead: Palm Jebel Ali was mothballed in 2008, revived in 2023, and Nakheel started launching villa plots in 2025. 2026 is the year the retail launches hit at scale.
palm-jebel-ali.body:
The first thing to know is that Palm Jebel Ali isn't Palm Jumeirah 2.0. The fronds are wider, the plots average 12,000 sq ft compared to Palm Jumeirah's 6,500, and the masterplan leans residential rather than hospitality-first. That changes who this island is for and how it will trade.

The pricing signal is the easy headline: launch prices are roughly 30% below current Palm Jumeirah frond averages. But the payment plan tells you more than the sticker. Nakheel structured a 50/50 plan with 40% of that during construction — a heavy front-load that filters out anyone hoping to flip pre-handover. This is deliberate.

When a developer front-loads the schedule, they're signalling two things. First, they want committed buyers who won't need liquidity for four years. Second, they want to fund construction from the sales, which reduces their reliance on debt and improves their ability to hold pricing on later phases.

If the historical comparison plays out, this is interesting. Palm Jumeirah frond villas launched at roughly 2,500 AED/sq ft in 2006, dipped below 1,800 in 2010, and now trade between 5,500 and 7,000. Palm Jebel Ali is opening at around 1,800. A 5–7× compound over 15 years is the thesis. It's not guaranteed — nothing about property is — but the setup is comparable to Palm Jumeirah's early cycle.

The risk is completion. Palm Jebel Ali's original 2008 delivery was cancelled and buyers were refunded years later. Nakheel's track record since 2010 on Palm Jumeirah is strong, but the first three years of Palm Jebel Ali will be the market's test of whether the second attempt is different.

My view: this isn't a first UAE investment. It's a portfolio position for someone who already owns a ready asset and wants long-duration exposure. 15–20% of a real-estate allocation is a reasonable size. If you're buying your first Dubai property, buy something ready first, live in the market for two cycles, then look at Palm Jebel Ali.

emaar-beachfront-payment.title: Emaar Beachfront — reading the payment plan behind the launch prices
emaar-beachfront-payment.excerpt: Every developer's payment plan tells you who they want in the building. Here's what Emaar Beachfront's says in 2026.
emaar-beachfront-payment.lead: Emaar Beachfront launched in 2019 at roughly 1,800 AED/sq ft. The first towers handed over in 2022 at 2,400. The 2026 launches are pricing at 2,600–2,800. But the payment plan has moved further than the price.
emaar-beachfront-payment.body:
Every launch has a payment plan and every payment plan is a filter. Emaar's early Beachfront releases were 40/60 — 40% during construction, 60% at handover. Retail-friendly. It signalled they wanted volume and didn't yet know how quickly the location would appreciate.

By 2022 that had shifted to 50/50. Same rough structure, but more front-loaded. By 2024, most premium buildings were on 60/40. And the newest premium releases in 2026 are 70/30 — the buyer funds nearly the entire construction before receiving keys.

Read against the market, this is confidence. Emaar has decided they don't need retail-friendly plans to sell. They can filter for capital-rich buyers who don't need financing leverage to complete. Those buyers hold longer, don't panic on cycle dips, and don't dump onto the resale market at handover.

What does this mean if you're buying today? The 2028-handover premium units are the tightest inventory in the peninsula. Priority allocation only, and the payment plan effectively demands a cash buyer. If you can meet the plan, the entry point is genuinely better than the resale market — even after two years of appreciation.

What it means if you're waiting to see: don't. The launch discount has compressed to under 10% versus comparable ready units. Emaar Beachfront now trades as a mature product. Buy for lifestyle or capital preservation, not opportunistic yield.

The short version — Emaar Beachfront is done being a growth story. It's now a defensive holding in the Dubai luxury market. That's not a criticism. Defensive positions have their place.

golden-visa-property.title: Dubai golden visa via property — what actually qualifies
golden-visa-property.excerpt: The AED 2M threshold is well-known. What most articles skip is what counts as "property" and how the visa timeline actually works.
golden-visa-property.lead: The AED 2M investment-based golden visa is the most common route for property buyers. Most first-time buyers understand the threshold. Fewer understand the qualifying details — and those details are where deals fall over.
golden-visa-property.body:
The rule as advertised: buy property worth AED 2M or more, get a 10-year residence visa. Spouse and children included. Renewable indefinitely as long as ownership is maintained.

The first nuance is location. Only freehold-zone properties qualify. Leasehold in older districts does not. If you're buying resale in a non-freehold community, the visa doesn't unlock — regardless of the purchase price.

The second nuance is the mortgage rule. You can buy a mortgaged property and still qualify, but your down-payment (equity in the deal) must be at least AED 2M. On a 10M purchase, a 20% down-payment is 2M — that works. On a 4M purchase, a 20% down-payment is 800K — that doesn't. You either pay more down or you need a larger property.

The third nuance is aggregation. You can combine two or more freehold properties to hit 2M in equity. This matters for buyers who want to spread across communities rather than concentrate.

The fourth is off-plan. Under-construction properties count, but only once you've paid at least 40% of the total purchase price. This is DLD's rule, and it's non-negotiable. A 60/40 payment plan won't qualify you until you're at handover.

The fifth is timeline. From completed purchase to visa issuance is typically 30–45 days. Family visas are processed alongside. Renewal every 10 years is administrative, not requalifying — as long as you still own qualifying property.

Common pitfalls I see: leasehold properties assumed to qualify (they don't), off-plan units expected to trigger the visa immediately (they won't until the 40% threshold is met), and buyers who split 2M across a freehold and a leasehold unit (only the freehold portion counts). Always confirm freehold status with the Dubai Land Department before signing an MoU.

off-plan-3-metrics.title: The three metrics I check before recommending an off-plan unit
off-plan-3-metrics.excerpt: Before I say yes to a client on any off-plan unit, three numbers have to check out. Payment plan, floor-plan efficiency, resale liquidity.
off-plan-3-metrics.lead: Every off-plan pitch comes with beautiful renders and a compelling story. Behind the marketing, three numbers tell you whether the unit will actually work as an investment. I don't recommend anything without them.
off-plan-3-metrics.body:
The first metric is payment-plan front-loading. If you're paying more than 60% before handover, you're funding the developer's construction against their timeline. That's fine if the developer has a track record of hitting delivery dates. Ask for their last five projects and check the actual handover dates against the original announced ones. A one-year slip is normal. Two years is a warning. Three years is a stop.

The second metric is floor-plan efficiency — the ratio of usable interior area to total sold area. In prime Dubai stock, 85% or higher is excellent. 75–85% is average. Below 75% is a red flag. Corner units and top-floor units tend to have worse ratios because of setback rules and mechanical spaces. Ask for the floor plan with both the total and usable areas marked. Reputable developers provide this; the ones who don't are the ones you should walk from.

The third metric is resale liquidity in the community. Look at the historic turnover in comparable units — same community, similar bed count, similar size. If there are 200 units in the tower and only 4 have traded in the last 12 months, plan for a longer exit. That's not necessarily a bad investment, but you need to buy at a price that accounts for illiquidity.

When these three align — priority allocation with a reasonable payment plan, 85%+ efficiency, and a proven resale market — I recommend without hesitation. That's the profile of the units I place with clients.

When they don't align — front-loaded payment on a first-time developer with tight floor plans in an untested community — that's a hard no. It doesn't matter how good the renders are.

The most common mistake I see buyers make is falling in love with the marketing. Every launch produces gorgeous imagery. Every developer's brochure claims record demand. The three metrics tell you what will actually happen five years from now — and five years is what matters.

offplan.body: Priority allocations with tier-one developers — Emaar, Meraas, Nakheel, Sobha, DAMAC — including early-access floor plans, launch pricing and staged payment plans.

included: Direct developer allocations at launch prices
Modelled yield & exit for each unit shortlisted
process: Written brief — budget, horizon, exit criteria
Modelled shortlist — 3 to 5 units with numbers

resale.forWho: Buyers who want ready keys, sellers who need a discreet exit, and families relocating on a fixed handover window.
process: Comparable analysis on each unit shown

management.intro: Financial arrangements and capital introductions for developers — from bridge financing on mid-project shortfalls to equity partners for new launches.
management.forWho: Developers, project sponsors, and land-holders with a capital gap between construction and delivery.

page.ctaBody: Send a short brief and I'll tell you if I can help — and be honest if I can't.

1.quote: Mathieu ran the numbers three different ways before recommending a single unit. He was the only advisor who told me the yield model didn't work — and then found one that did.
1.author: Private investor

2.quote: Straight-talking, French-fluent, and knows every off-plan launch coming out of Emaar before it hits the market. Refreshing after two years of noise.
2.author: HNW family office

3.quote: We needed bridge financing for a mid-project shortfall. Mathieu introduced us to three viable capital partners inside a week. Closed with the second one.
3.author: UAE developer

contact.title: Let's talk about Dubai.
contact.subtitle: A short WhatsApp is usually the fastest way to reach me. Emails are answered within the working day.

form.name: Your name
form.messagePlaceholder: e.g. Looking to place 20M in off-plan across Dubai Harbour and Palm Jebel Ali over the next 12 months — need a shortlist and a payment-plan comparison.

errors.name: Please tell me your name.
errors.email: Please add an email so I can reply.
errors.message: Tell me a bit about what you're looking for.
errors.messageShort: A few more words please — 20 characters minimum.

success.title: Thank you.
success.body: Your message is on its way. I read every enquiry personally and reply within one working day — usually much sooner.

error.title: Something went wrong.
error.body: The message couldn't be delivered. Please try again in a moment, or reach me on WhatsApp — that's the fastest path.

modal.title: Let's talk about Dubai.
modal.subtitle: A short brief is all I need to reply with something useful.

page.title: Send a brief, get a considered answer.
page.intro: WhatsApp is the fastest way to reach me. Emails are answered within the working day. First meetings are on video call, or in person in Dubai by appointment.

whatsapp.label: WhatsApp
whatsapp.detail: Fastest response — usually within two hours during UAE working hours.

email.label: Email
email.detail: Best for a detailed brief with attachments. Modelled shortlists come back as PDFs.

call.label: Voice call
call.detail: After a few messages — 30 minutes on Zoom, Google Meet or WhatsApp.

meet.label: In person
meet.detail: Dubai, by appointment. Property viewings arranged directly with owners.

page.briefHeading: What to include in your first message.
page.briefIntro: The more of this you can share up front, the faster I can reply with something useful. Approximate is fine — precision beats vagueness, but rough beats nothing.

budget.label: Budget
budget.detail: Approximate is fine. A range beats a single number.

horizon.label: Horizon
horizon.detail: Hold period, exit trigger, cash-flow needs.

locations.label: Locations
locations.detail: Communities you know, ones you'd like me to shortlist.

constraints.label: Constraints
constraints.detail: Visa timing, school year, business setup, family logistics.

page.formSubtitle: Same fields as WhatsApp. Choose whichever channel you prefer once you're done.
page.responseBody: WhatsApp: usually within two hours during UAE working hours (Sun–Thu, 09:00–19:00 GST). Email: same working day. Weekends and public holidays: reply Sunday morning.

footer.placeholderNote: Personal profile of Mathieu Poissonnet. Stat figures on this page are illustrative pending final review. Bio and contact information sourced from a public LinkedIn profile.
